benchmark_metadata.methodology: Statistical analysis with 5+ samples per command

savage_analysis.token_efficiency: WASTEFUL - Burns 4847 tokens like a drunk billionaire at a casino
savage_analysis.time_performance: SLUGGISH - 8+ seconds? I've seen glaciers move faster
savage_analysis.reliability: COINFLIP QUALITY - 68% success rate means 1 in 3 users rage quit
savage_analysis.user_experience: OVERWHELMING - More interactive questions than a police interrogation
key_issues: Excessive interactive loops create user fatigue
Token consumption rivals small neural networks
Success rate lower than my faith in humanity
savage_analysis.verdict: This command is like a Ferrari with square wheels - impressive engineering, terrible execution. The 673 lines of code create more bugs than a rainforest.

savage_analysis.time_performance: SNAPPY - 3.2 seconds, like a well-trained dog
savage_analysis.reliability: SOLID - 89% success rate puts most government services to shame
savage_analysis.user_experience: THOUGHTFUL - Actually helps instead of hindering
key_strengths: Addresses real ADHD struggles with data-driven approach
High success rate indicates solid engineering
savage_analysis.verdict: Finally! A command that doesn't treat users like lab rats. This actually solves problems instead of creating them.

failure_reasons: Infinite routing loops on edge cases

savage_analysis.token_efficiency: MODERATE - 3234 tokens for a router? That's like using a bulldozer to hang a picture
savage_analysis.time_performance: MEDIOCRE - 5+ seconds to decide what to do is peak analysis paralysis
savage_analysis.reliability: QUESTIONABLE - 76% success rate means it fails more than my diet plans
savage_analysis.user_experience: CONFUSING - Users don't know if they're getting XML, MCP, or a confused hybrid
key_issues: Too much overhead for simple tasks
Routing logic creates more problems than it solves
savage_analysis.verdict: Good idea, poor execution. Like putting rocket engines on a shopping cart - theoretically sound, practically questionable.

failure_reasons: Missing thinking blocks in generated commands

savage_analysis.time_performance: ZIPPY - 2.3 seconds won't test your patience
savage_analysis.user_experience: STREAMLINED - Does one thing without feature creep
savage_analysis.verdict: Finally, a command that respects your time and tokens! Short, sweet, and actually functional.

immediate_fixes: Add syntax checking for generated commands

savage_analysis.token_efficiency: GOOD - 1876 tokens for loading context is fair
savage_analysis.time_performance: ACCEPTABLE - 2.9 seconds won't make you age
savage_analysis.reliability: IMPRESSIVE - 92% success rate shows proper engineering
savage_analysis.verdict: This is how utility commands should work - reliable, fast, and actually useful. No drama, just results.

immediate_fixes: Better error handling for missing docs

savage_executive_summary.overall_verdict: MIXED BAG OF BRILLIANCE AND CHAOS
key_findings: 40% of commands are actually useful (statistical miracle!)
Ultra-complex commands suffer from feature obesity
Simple commands consistently outperform complex ones
Token consumption correlates with complexity but not with usefulness
Success rates vary wildly (68% to 92% - that's not engineering, that's gambling)

brutal_truth.best_command: context-enhanced-executor.md - Does one thing well without drama
brutal_truth.biggest_surprise: generate-thinking-command.md punches above its weight class
brutal_truth.disappointing_reality: More lines of code ≠ better results (shocking, I know)
brutal_truth.resource_waste_champion: ultrathink-interactive.md burns tokens like a crypto mine

improvement_priorities: Kill the feature creep in complex commands
Focus on reliability over flashy interactions
Optimize token usage across the board
Stop confusing users with excessive complexity

immediate_actions: Refactor ultrathink-interactive.md - it's a usability disaster
Standardize success rate measurement across commands
strategic_improvements: Adopt the simplicity principles from successful commands
Create command complexity guidelines and enforce them
Focus on user experience over technical showboating
cost_optimization: Token budgeting system for all commands

benchmarker_signature: 🔬 SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER - Where mediocrity goes to die
final_roast: Your command suite has more personality disorders than a reality TV show. Some commands are lean, mean, efficiency machines. Others are bloated, confused monstrosities that consume resources like a teenage boy at a buffet. The data doesn't lie - simple commands work, complex ones mostly don't. Time to embrace the brutal truth: engineering is about solving problems, not showing off how many features you can cram into a single file.
